upgrade_policy.failure_visibility: fail closed and keep the previously known-good install state

compatibility_questions: Which contract versions are installed for each declared entry?
Which executable path should the host invoke?
Which install layout manifest defines the minimum bundled runtime/resources?
Which runtime_state does the host currently observe?
If the runtime is blocked, what fail-closed reason is visible to the operator?
Should the host assert LOOM_RUNTIME_SCENE=upgrade-rehearsal for this run?
Does the host need to refresh referenced resources after upgrade?
Did the registry gain or retire scenario entries that the host must rediscover?
Does the installed story intake surface still keep User Story, Story Readiness, and Story Business Confirmation separate from Work Item, spec, plan, recovery, review, merge, and closeout state?
Does the installed shared reference layer still expose Work Item as the unique execution entry?
Does the installed gate chain still expose spec gate, build gate, review gate, and merge gate without parallel aliases?
Does the installed status control plane still converge fact-chain and host control signals without adding authored state?
Does the installed profile maturity model still preserve light -> standard -> strong upgrades?
Does the installed merge surface still treat host merge as GitHub-controlled rather than Loom-authored?
